get.description:
Liveness only — static by design. Readiness (DB + storage) is /ready; point container
and platform healthchecks THERE, or an unreachable database still reports healthy.

Readiness: can this instance do real work? Probes the database and the storage volume
so the orchestrator restarts/alerts on a data-layer outage instead of serving green.
Tappable login tiles for the kiosk — active users only, no secrets.

schema.title: Response Roster Api Auth Roster Get
Response Logout Api Auth Logout Post

get.description: ALL login users incl. inactive — the admin members table.

schema.title: Response List Users Api Auth Users Get

get.description:
Let the /admin UI choose its first screen without poking a protected endpoint:
whether admin is configured at all, and whether THIS browser already holds a valid
admin session. Never leaks the secret.

schema.title: Response Admin Session State Api Admin Session Get
Response Admin Login Api Admin Login Post
Response Admin Logout Api Admin Logout Post

get.description:
PUBLIC (no auth) — the login screen needs branding before login.

Last-good fallback: if the persisted ``config_json`` is missing or fails validation
(e.g. a hand-edited bad row), serve a safe empty config and log a warning. Never raises.

put.description:
Admin-only. Validates the body (422 on invalid), persists the document to the
singleton row, stamps ``updated_by`` (the admin's user when driving the UI, NULL for
a CLI push), and returns the same projection as GET.

get.description:
Admin-only audit metadata for the singleton config row: when it was last saved
and who saved it (resolved display name). Returns plain nulls on a fresh / unstamped row.

schema.title: Response Get Config Meta Api Config Meta Get

get.description:
PUBLIC (no auth) — the login screen needs the logo/favicon before sign-in.

SECURITY: only serve keys under the ``branding/`` prefix and reject any traversal
sequence so this endpoint can't be turned into an arbitrary-file read.

schema.title: Response List Incidents Api Incidents Get
Response List People Api Incidents  Incident Id  People Get
Response Replace People Api Incidents  Incident Id  People Put
Response List Notes Api Incidents  Incident Id  Notes Get
Response Upload Media Api Incidents  Incident Id  Media Post

post.description: Receive an alarm. Secret via ?secret= or X-Webhook-Secret. 200 even on duplicate.

schema.title: Response Webhook Api Divera Webhook Post
Response Pool Api Divera Pool Get
Response Refresh Api Divera Pool Refresh Post
Response Archive Api Divera Pool  Divera Id  Delete

get.description: The crew roster, ordered by name. Active-only unless ``include_inactive=true``.

schema.title: Response List Personnel Api Personnel Get

post.description: Manually add a crew member (hand entry; ``divera_id`` normally null).

delete.description: Deactivate (never hard-delete) — old incidents/reports keep resolving names.

schema.title: Response Deactivate Person Api Personnel  Person Id  Delete

post.description:
Import a UTF-8 CSV (header row required). Columns: ``name`` (→ display_name,
required), ``divera_id`` (int, optional) and ``rank`` (optional — key/label/abbr of a
configured Dienstgrad, mapped to a rank key). Upsert by divera_id when present, else
create. Bad rows are skipped and collected into ``errors``.

schema.title: Response Import Csv Api Personnel Import Csv Post
Response Traccar Status Api Traccar Status Get
Response Positions Api Traccar Positions Get
Response Trails Api Traccar Trails Get
Response Geocode Search Api Geocode Search Get

get.description: Nearest registered address to a map-clicked point; null on no match/failure.

schema.title: Response Geocode Reverse Api Geocode Reverse Get

get.description: Global datasets only (symbols + geodata); per-object plans live under /objects.

schema.title: Response List Reference Api Reference Get

post.description:
Delete every ``checklists:*`` reference dataset whose id is NOT in ``keep`` (the manifest's
current template + asset ids), so a renamed or removed checklist doesn't leave a ghost dataset
the Checkliste surface would still fetch. Called by ``admin_checklists`` after a push. Admin-gated
(the push client holds only the admin-session cookie, so it can't use the CurrentUser listing).

schema.title: Response Prune Checklists Api Reference Checklists Prune Post

post.description: Trigger an auto-fetch (SharePoint/Graph sync) — designed-for-later, not wired yet.

schema.title: Response Fetch Reference Api Reference  Dataset Id  Fetch Post
Response List Objects Api Objects Get
Response Objects Near Incident Api Incidents  Incident Id  Objects Get
Response List Events Api Incidents  Incident Id  Events Get

post.description:
Flush a batch of client tactical events (entity.*, draw.*, layer.toggle, undo, redo).

Server assigns seq + recorded_at + chain hash; client supplies occurred_at (scene
wall-clock, possibly buffered offline).

schema.title: Response Ingest Events Api Incidents  Incident Id  Events Post

get.description:
Nearest workspace snapshot with occurred_at <= `at` — the replay fold anchor.

Read-only; any authenticated user. Returns found=false (no workspace) when every
snapshot is in the future, so the client folds from an empty earliest state.

Vehicle GPS samples in the window (for vehicle replay). May be empty today —
the Traccar→samples capture job isn't wired yet (PLAN-audit-trail §4, Phase 6).

schema.title: Response Samples Api Incidents  Incident Id  Samples Get

get.description:
Server-side convenience reconstruction at `at`: nearest snapshot + events since.

The scrubber folds locally per-frame (fetching snapshot+events once); this single
round-trip is for non-interactive consumers (export, debugging). Read-only.

schema.title: Response State At Api Incidents  Incident Id  State Get
Response Verify Api Incidents  Incident Id  Verify Get

get.description: All rows with seq > since_seq, oldest first. since_seq=0 → the full journal.

post.description:
Append a batch of rows. Idempotent on the client row id: rows this incident already
holds are skipped silently, so an offline outbox may retry the same batch after a lost
response without duplicating the record. Returns the accepted rows with their seqs.

get.description: The deployment's VAPID public key — null while push is not configured.

schema.title: Response Vapid Key Api Push Vapid Key Get
Response Subscribe Api Push Subscriptions Post

get.description:
Admin-only maintenance/status snapshot. Resilient: each section is guarded
so a partial failure returns that section as null/error and the rest fine.

schema.title: Response Get System Api System Get

post.description: Log a client-reported error at WARNING (visible without DEBUG). Never raises.

ClientError.title: ClientError
ClientError.description: A bounded report of a frontend error. All fields optional/length-capped.

ConfigIntegrations.title: ConfigIntegrations
ConfigIntegrations.description:
Env-derived integration availability (the GET projection's `integrations`).

These are FACTS about the deployment's secrets, NOT the document's on/off intent —
hence the `*Configured` naming. The frontend should read these to decide whether to
surface Divera/Traccar features. (docs/CONFIGURATION.md §1 names the document's intent
fields `diveraEnabled`/`traccarEnabled`; the GET output deliberately uses
`diveraConfigured`/`traccarConfigured` to signal env-derived truth.)

DeploymentConfigIn.title: DeploymentConfigIn
DeploymentConfigIn.description:
The full config document an admin PUTs. All sections optional → `{}` is valid.

The input `integrations` block (ON/OFF intent) is accepted but IGNORED for the GET
projection: integration availability is derived from env-configured secrets, not the
document (see ConfigIntegrations / config_projection in the router).

DeploymentConfigOut.title: DeploymentConfigOut
DeploymentConfigOut.description:
GET/PUT response projection: the validated document PLUS env-derived integration
flags. NEVER includes updated_by, raw secrets, or API keys.

DiveraTakeBody.title: DiveraTakeBody
DiveraTakeBody.description:
Optional EL corrections applied when taking a pool alarm into an incident.

All fields optional: an empty body takes the alarm verbatim (backwards-compatible),
any field present overrides the mirrored Divera value. The wizard sends the reviewed
fields so a wrong address/keyword/pin is fixed before the incident is born.

FleetAttributeList.title: FleetAttributeList
FleetAttributeList.description:
A data-driven suggestion list for one symbol field (``field == 'title'`` targets the
title input; any other key targets that detail row). Replaces the fixed vehicle/Lüfter/…
lists below so a deployment can attach a list to any symbol field.

IncidentMeta.title: IncidentMeta
IncidentMeta.description: List/metadata view — never carries the workspace blob.

JournalAppendIn.title: JournalAppendIn
JournalAppendIn.description:
Batch of Verlauf rows (frontend TimelineEvent dicts, stored verbatim). The row's
own `id` is the idempotency key; a 32 KB per-row cap keeps a bad client from turning
the journal into blob storage (photos/audio go through /media, never in rows).

MittelConfig.title: MittelConfig
MittelConfig.description:
Station-wide material-use config: the Mittel catalogue, the optional source list, and
common unit suggestions for custom («Anderes Mittel») entries. All optional → the app falls
back to its national defaults (empty catalogue, free-typed materials).

MittelItem.title: MittelItem
MittelItem.description:
One station-catalogue material: id + label + default unit (Stk/l/Sack/m/Flasche …),
an optional grouping `category`, and an optional per-source `stock` (nominal load-out +
where it lives → drives the used/available readout and the Bestand overview).

MittelSource.title: MittelSource
MittelSource.description: One source a material can be drawn from (vehicle / depot / …).

MittelStockEntry.title: MittelStockEntry
MittelStockEntry.description: How many of a material are normally carried at one source (the standard load-out).

ModuleConfig.title: ModuleConfig
ModuleConfig.description:
One Objektplan module — drives BOTH the app's plan tile and the import parsing, so a
station configures its module set / labels / parsing in one place.

Display: ``code`` is the short tile label ('M1', '2/3', 'Wasser'), plus title/subtitle/
orientation/order/icon. Parsing: ``match`` is a regex tested (case-insensitive) against a
source PDF's filename stem — the first module whose ``match`` hits claims the file.
``combinedWith`` marks a combined sheet that also fills other slots (modul2-3 → modul2 +
modul3). ``family`` marks a generative module whose ``match`` has a capture group for a
sub-slot — "Modul 5 - Wasser" → ``modul5-wasser`` labelled "Wasser".

PersonnelCreate.title: PersonnelCreate
PersonnelCreate.description:
Manually add a crew member. ``divera_id`` is normally null (hand entry); a bare
name is enough.

PersonnelSyncPreview.title: PersonnelSyncPreview
PersonnelSyncPreview.description: Read-only diff of Divera members vs the personnel table (no writes applied).

RankConfig.title: RankConfig
RankConfig.description:
One Dienstgrad in the station's ordered rank list. Position in ``roster.ranks`` is
the seniority order (most senior first). ``tier`` drives the "nur Offiziere" picker
filter and the Anwesenheit grouping; ``abbr`` is the short badge shown in lists.

ReferenceLayerConfig.title: ReferenceLayerConfig
ReferenceLayerConfig.description:
A station-supplied reference layer (raster WMS/WMTS or vector GeoJSON).

Permissive (extra=ignore) but enforces the kind→payload invariant: raster layers
must carry `tiles`, geojson layers must carry `geojson`. Malformed entries reject.

RosterUser.title: RosterUser
RosterUser.description: A login tile — never includes anything secret.

SnapshotOut.title: SnapshotOut
SnapshotOut.description: The nearest workspace snapshot <= a requested instant — the fold anchor.

UserAdminOut.title: UserAdminOut
UserAdminOut.description: Full admin view of a login user (incl. inactive). No secrets — never pin_hash.
